sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Stade Brestois have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, displaying resilience with late goals in key games. Auxerre, on the other hand, are struggling, managing only 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses in their recent matches, conceding late goals in multiple fixtures.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, matches between Stade Brestois and Auxerre have been competitive, with a 2-2 draw and a 3-0 win for Auxerre. Both games saw goals from both sides in one instance, suggesting potential for an open contest.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Stade Brestois, sitting 11th with 19 points, have scored 21 goals but conceded 27, indicating defensive vulnerabilities. Auxerre, in 16th with 12 points, have struggled offensively with just 14 goals while conceding 25, highlighting their difficulty in securing results.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Brest's Romain Del Castillo (6 goals, 2 assists) will be crucial, while missing players like Kamory Doumbia could impact midfield creativity. Auxerre miss key forward Lassine Sinayoko (6 goals), weakening their attack, but goalkeeper Théo De Percin has been in form with an 8.7 rating in recent games.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Brest’s strength in aerial duels (52.5% won, 4th in league) and pressing (5.25 possessions won in final third per game) could exploit Auxerre’s defensive frailties. Auxerre’s lack of clean sheets in recent matches (4 games) and tendency to concede first (7/9 games) might give Brest an early edge.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Brest’s home advantage, better league position, and Sofascore’s 46% home win probability, they are slight favorites. Auxerre’s poor form and missing key attackers tilt the balance towards a narrow victory for Brest, though a draw remains plausible at 27% probability.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Stade Brestois leads Auxerre with a narrow 1-0 scoreline at Stade Francis-Le Ble. The home side capitalized on their opportunity through a well-taken goal by Ludovic Ajorque in the 33rd minute. Brest has shown a slight edge in possession and created more dangerous moments in the final third. Auxerre, despite having their moments, struggled to convert their chances, missing a big opportunity in the first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance
halftimeSections.text: Stade Brestois has been more dominant in duels, particularly in the air, showcasing their physicality. Their frequent entries into the penalty area and ability to win possession in advanced positions have put Auxerre under pressure. On the other hand, Auxerre has shown resilience but lacked precision in the final third, with fewer entries into dangerous zones and a notable miss that could have leveled the game. Defensive solidity has been a strength for Brest, with key clearances and tackles keeping Auxerre at bay.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players
halftimeSections.text: Ludovic Ajorque has been the standout for Brest, not only scoring the opener but also posing a constant threat upfront. Remy Labeau Lascary has also played a crucial role with an assist and involvement in creating chances. For Auxerre, players like Ibrahim Osman have shown intent but failed to capitalize on a big chance. The away side will need more from their attacking players to turn the game around in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Auxerre started brightly with several attacking spells in the opening minutes, pushing forward with intent. However, Brest gradually took control around the midway point of the half, stringing together consistent attacking phases, culminating in their goal. Towards the end of the half, Auxerre mounted a late push to equalize, showing renewed energy in their forward play, but Brest managed to hold firm.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: For Stade Brestois, maintaining their composure and continuing to exploit spaces in Auxerre’s defense will be key. They could look to increase their tempo to create more openings. Auxerre, meanwhile, must improve their finishing and make the most of their final third entries. Reducing defensive errors and tightening up in duels will be critical if they are to mount a comeback in the second half.
